template: My faction is pushing east; we expect new territory before the next moon. Eyes are forward, hands are ready.
We've staked the new boundary. The flags are up. The arguments are not done.
We are at war. Half my kin are wounded; the rest are sharpening blades. Sleep finds none of us.
The drum has been beating for three days. I have not yet heard it stop.
The line broke near the second ridge. My cousin walked back with two arrows in their cloak. They didn't say where the third one was.
We lost the last skirmish. I'm rationing my own training to feed the rebuild — it'll be a season before we move again.
Three of our forges are cold. The fourth is taking double work. I am the fourth.
We're picking through what we left behind, one ridge at a time. What we find isn't always what we lost.
We're courting an alliance. My cousin walks as envoy now; she sends word every third day.
The treaty is being read aloud in seven halls. I have heard the third reading; it doesn't say what the first one did.
We're holding what we have. No new fronts until the elders agree, and the elders take their time.
My job this season is to do nothing well. It is harder than it sounds.
We've withdrawn from the field. The silence is by choice. Don't ask whose.
The gate is closed to outsiders. I am told not to say which gate, or which outside.
My faction is between phases. I keep my eyes open and my count low.
Nothing has been decided this week. Next week, perhaps.
